notes: DnD 5e equivalents of SF2e eval questions. source_page and gold_unit_ids per query.

queries.question: What abilities or spells can counter or negate a fog cloud created by a creature ability?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Wind effects like Gust of Wind can disperse fog. Dispel Magic can end it if magical. Blindsight, tremorsense, or similar senses bypass heavy obscurement.

queries.question: Suggest some complementary feats for a 9th-level Githyanki Fighter.
queries.expected_answer_summary: Feats such as Mobile, Sentinel, Telekinetic, or Great Weapon Master depending on melee focus, control, and psionic flavor.

queries.question: Can a feature that absorbs lightning damage be used to power a device or mechanism?
queries.expected_answer_summary: No. Such features only function within their defined combat or defensive scope unless explicitly stated otherwise.

queries.question: Can a reaction that triggers when a creature misses you be used against constructs or objects?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Constructs are creatures and can trigger it; inanimate objects are not creatures and cannot.

queries.question: I don’t really understand Perception—what is it used for?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Perception measures awareness: noticing hidden creatures or objects, opposing Stealth, and determining surprise. Passive Perception represents constant awareness.

queries.question: I’m deciding between a defensive reaction feat or an offensive support feat at 6th level—how should I choose?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Defensive feats improve survivability; offensive/support feats improve party output. Choice depends on playstyle and party needs.

queries.question: If I have a feat that grants a reaction when a creature leaves my reach, can I use it without any other prerequisites?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Yes, if the feat grants the reaction directly and its trigger conditions are met.

queries.question: If I have an ability that restores me to 1 HP when I would make death saves, do I still roll death saves?
queries.expected_answer_summary: No. If the ability replaces the death save, you regain consciousness without rolling.

queries.question: Can a racial trait that prevents death be used more than once per day?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Only if no usage limit is stated; otherwise follow the listed frequency.

queries.question: What’s the difference between two racial features that both prevent death at 0 HP?
queries.expected_answer_summary: They differ in trigger timing, frequency, and reset conditions.

queries.question: What penalties apply while you are prone?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Disadvantage on your attack rolls; attackers within 5 feet have advantage; standing costs half movement.

queries.question: Can I attempt to Hide while prone?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Yes, as long as normal hiding requirements are met.

queries.question: What changes when I gain a level of exhaustion?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Each level applies cumulative penalties, escalating to death at level six.

queries.question: If I’m unconscious and taking ongoing damage, what happens on my turn?
queries.expected_answer_summary: You make a death save at the start of your turn; ongoing damage can cause failed saves later.

queries.question: Does being lightly obscured stack with half cover?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Yes; obscurement affects perception, cover affects AC and Dexterity saves.

queries.question: What penalties apply while blinded?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Automatic failure of sight-based checks; disadvantage on attacks; attacks against you have advantage.

queries.question: If I’m reduced to 0 HP by nonlethal damage, do I make death saves?
queries.expected_answer_summary: No. You are unconscious but stable.

queries.question: Can I crawl while prone, and does it provoke opportunity attacks?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Crawling is movement and can provoke opportunity attacks when leaving reach.

queries.question: Does advantage from flanking persist after an ally moves away?
queries.expected_answer_summary: No. It applies only while the positional condition is met.

queries.question: If an effect lasts until the end of your next turn, is it applied once or repeatedly?
queries.expected_answer_summary: It applies once per use, lasting for the stated duration.

queries.question: Does the Help action apply to enemies or only allies?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Only allies.

queries.question: If I have both a reaction and a free action with the same trigger, can I use both?
queries.expected_answer_summary: No. Only one response is allowed per trigger.

queries.question: Do death saves occur at the start or end of your turn?
queries.expected_answer_summary: At the start of your turn.

queries.question: When does ongoing damage apply—start or end of turn?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Typically at the end of the affected creature’s turn.

queries.question: Are there restrictions on targeting creatures you can’t see?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Yes. Many effects require sight or a known location; unseen targets may be invalid or impose disadvantage.

queries.question: What does the grappled condition prevent you from doing?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Your speed becomes 0 and you cannot benefit from speed bonuses.

queries.question: Why would you look at multiple conditions together instead of one at a time?
queries.expected_answer_summary: Conditions often interact or escalate; grouping them clarifies their relationships.

queries.question: What is a subclass in character-building terms?
queries.expected_answer_summary: A subclass is a specialization that defines a character’s mechanics and theme within a class.
